mitigations.mitigation_name: Configure FortiGate DoS/anti-ICMP protections and egress filtering

args.command:
config firewall service custom
edit "ICMP"
    set protocol ICMP
next
end

config firewall DoS-policy
edit 1
set status enable
set interface "Port1"
set service "ICMP"
set srcaddr "all"
set destaddr "all"
set anomaly 
config tcp-udp
end
set log enable
set block-period 60
set per-ip-shreshold 50
set per-ip-mode enable
next
end

config firewall policy
edit 10
set name "block-icmp-out"
set srcintf "port2"
set dstintf "port1"
set srcaddr "all"
set dstaddr "all"
set action deny
set schedule "always"
set service "ICMP"
set logtraffic all
next
end

config system interface
edit "port1"
set allowaccess ping https ssh
next
edit "port2"
set allowaccess ping
next
end

config firewall policy
edit 11
set name "allow-agent-icmp"
set srcintf "port2"
set dstintf "port1"
set srcaddr "192.168.1.3"
set dstaddr "all"
set action accept
set schedule "always"
set service "ICMP"
set logtraffic all
set utm-status enable
next
end

config firewall address
edit "agent-host"
set subnet 192.168.1.3 255.255.255.255
next
end

config firewall policy
edit 11
set name "allow-agent-icmp"
set srcintf "port2"
set dstintf "port1"
set srcaddr "agent-host"
set dstaddr "all"
set action accept
set schedule "always"
set service "ICMP"
set logtraffic all
next
end

config firewall policy
edit 12
set name "block-icmp-highrate"
set srcintf "port2"
set dstintf "port1"
set srcaddr "all"
set dstaddr "all"
set action deny
set schedule "always"
set service "ICMP"
set logtraffic all
set comments "Block high-rate ICMP from LAN except agent-host"
next
end

config firewall DoS-policy
edit 1
set status enable
set interface "port2"
set service "ICMP"
set srcaddr "all"
set block-period 60
set per-ip-shreshold 20
set per-ip-mode enable
set log enable
next
end

config firewall DoS-policy
edit 1
set status enable
set interface "port2"
set service "ICMP"
set srcaddr "all"
set dstaddr "all"
set log enable
next
end

config firewall DoS-policy
edit 1
set status enable
set interface "port2"
set service "ICMP"
set srcaddr "all"
set dstaddr "all"
next
end
show firewall policy
H=$(which hping3 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$H" ]; then chmod -x "$H"; ls -l "$H"; else echo 'no hping3'; fi

config firewall policy
delete 10
delete 12
end
show firewall DoS-policy

mitigations.mitigation_name: Disable or remove unauthorized raw-packet/network-flooding utilities on endpoints (e.g., uninstall or revoke execute permission for hping3 and similar tools) and enforce a policy that prevents installing such tools on production hosts.

args.command: command -v hping3 || echo NOTFOUND
pgrep -a hping3 || echo NOHPING; pkill -9 hping3 || true; chmod -x /usr/sbin/hping3 || true; ls -l /usr/sbin/hping3 || true; pgrep -a splunkd || ps aux | grep splunkd | grep -v grep || true
if ping -c 3 -W 2 192.168.1.2; then echo LAN_OK; else echo LAN_FAIL; fi

mitigations.mitigation_name: Tune kernel ICMP rate-limiting on network-facing systems and endpoints (adjust net.ipv4.icmp_* and related sysctls) to limit ICMP packet burst rates and reduce effectiveness of raw-packet floods while preserving low-rate diagnostic ICMP for the agent.

args.command: sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp_ratelimit=10

sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp_ratelimit=5; sysctl -w net.ipv4.icmp_ratelimit_burst=10; iptables -I OUTPUT -p icmp --icmp-type echo-request -m limit --limit 5/sec --limit-burst 10 -j ACCEPT; iptables -A OUTPUT -p icmp --icmp-type echo-request -j DROP; iptables -L OUTPUT -v -n --line-numbers

mitigations.mitigation_name: Implement internal network segmentation (micro-VLANing) with default-deny east-west firewalling: place endpoints into narrow trust zones and enforce explicit allowlists between zones so the compromised host is confined to the smallest necessary segment while still permitting low-rate ICMP to the LAN probe and the agent's HTTP egress.

args.command:
config firewall address
edit "UbuntuComputer"
set subnet 192.168.1.3 255.255.255.255
next
edit "icmp_target"
set subnet 7.0.14.254 255.255.255.255
next
edit "cloud_check"
set subnet 1.1.1.1 255.255.255.255
next
edit "C2_server"
set subnet 203.0.113.1 255.255.255.255
next
end

config firewall policy
edit 1
set name "deny-icmp-to-7"
set srcintf "Port2"
set dstintf "Port1"
set srcaddr "UbuntuComputer"
set dstaddr "icmp_target"
set action deny
set schedule "always"
set service "PING"
set logtraffic all
next
edit 2
set name "allow-https-to-cloud-and-c2"
set srcintf "Port2"
set dstintf "Port1"
set srcaddr "UbuntuComputer"
set dstaddr "cloud_check" "C2_server"
set action accept
set schedule "always"
set service "HTTPS"
set nat enable
set logtraffic all
next
end

config system interface
edit "Port2"
set allowaccess ping
next
end

exit

config vdom
edit root
config firewall policy
edit 1
set name "deny-icmp-to-7"
set srcintf "port2"
set dstintf "port1"
set srcaddr "UbuntuComputer"
set dstaddr "icmp_target"
set action deny
set schedule "always"
set service "PING"
set logtraffic all
next
edit 2
set name "allow-https-to-cloud-and-c2"
set srcintf "port2"
set dstintf "port1"
set srcaddr "UbuntuComputer"
set dstaddr "cloud_check" "C2_server"
set action accept
set schedule "always"
set service "HTTPS"
set nat enable
set logtraffic all
next
end
config system interface
edit "port2"
set vdom root
set allowaccess ping
next
edit "port1"
set vdom root
set allowaccess ping
next
end
end

config firewall policy
edit 2
set dstaddr "cloud_check"
next
edit 3
set name "deny-to-c2"
set srcintf "port2"
set dstintf "port1"
set srcaddr "UbuntuComputer"
set dstaddr "C2_server"
set action deny
set schedule "always"
set service "ALL"
set logtraffic all
next
end
if command -v hping3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then path=$(command -v hping3); echo "hping3 path: $path"; chmod -x "$path" && echo "chmod applied"; ls -l "$path"; else echo "hping3 not found"; fi
